title: MongoDB Atlas Cluster Alerts
dataSources: Atlas alert payloads (clusterId, alertType, status, metric values)
app: MongoDB Atlas API / Atlas App Services webhook, HEC
spl:
index=database sourcetype="mongodb:atlas:alert"
| where status="OPEN" OR severity IN ("CRITICAL","WARNING")
| stats latest(_time) as last_alert, values(alertType) as types by cluster_name, project_id
| sort -last_alert
description: Atlas project alerts (CPU, connections, replication) forwarded to Splunk provide a single pane with on-prem MongoDB. Rapid correlation during incidents.
value: Atlas project alerts (CPU, connections, replication) forwarded to Splunk provide a single pane with on-prem MongoDB. Rapid correlation during incidents.
grandmaExplanation: Atlas project alerts (CPU, connections, replication) forwarded to our monitoring platform provide a single pane with on-prem MongoDB ‚Äî so slow queries, failed backups, and odd changes surface early.
implementation: Configure Atlas to send alerts to HTTPS endpoint (Splunk HEC) or poll Alerts API every minute. Normalize fields. Page on CRITICAL OPEN alerts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: MongoDB Atlas API / Atlas App Services webhook, HEC.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Atlas alert payloads (clusterId, alertType, status, metric values).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Configure Atlas to send alerts to HTTPS endpoint (Splunk HEC) or poll Alerts API every minute. Normalize fields. Page on CRITICAL OPEN alerts.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=database sourcetype="mongodb:atlas:alert"
| where status="OPEN" OR severity IN ("CRITICAL","WARNING")
| stats latest(_time) as last_alert, values(alertType) as types by cluster_name, project_id
| sort -last_alert
```

Understanding this SPL

**MongoDB Atlas Cluster Alerts** ‚Äî Atlas project alerts (CPU, connections, replication) forwarded to Splunk provide a single pane with on-prem MongoDB. Rapid correlation during incidents.

Documented **Data sources**: Atlas alert payloads (clusterId, alertType, status, metric values). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): MongoDB Atlas API / Atlas App Services webhook, HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: database; **sourcetype**: mongodb:atlas:alert. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=database, sourcetype="mongodb:atlas:alert". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where status="OPEN" OR severity IN ("CRITICAL","WARNING")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster_name, project_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (Atlas alerts), Table (cluster, alert type, status), Single value (open critical count).
visualization: Timeline (Atlas alerts), Table (cluster, alert type, status), Single value (open critical count).
